reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: All gates passed. Gate 2 (Trust Boundary): P2P gossip entry point is untrusted, no compromised component required. Gate 3 (Code Verification): Code reading is factually correct - cache key at gossipHandlers.ts:296 uses toRootHex(BeaconBlockHeader.hashTreeRoot()), which excludes the signature field from SignedBeaconBlockHeader. Cache hit at L300-308 returns early without executing validateGossipDataColumnSidecar (L317), bypassing signature verification at dataColumnSidecar.ts:126-137. Gate 4 (Exploitability): An attacker can trigger this via P2P gossip by sending DataColumnSidecar_1 with valid signature (cached), then DataColumnSidecar_2 with same header but forged signature (cache hit, validation skipped). The GossipSub validator (gossipValidatorFn.ts:115) returns Accept, causing the original invalid message to propagate to peers. Gate 5 (Spec Cross-Reference): 01e entry (outputs/01e_PARTIAL_W0B1_1771748647.json:824-841) requires 'Dedup cache hit must not bypass signature verification on resubmitted items with different proofs' with assertion 'forall item, seen_set: item.id in seen_set => verify_signature(item.sig) must still execute on new instance'. The code clearly violates this invariant. Severity calibration: Original HIGH downgraded to Medium because impact is limited to one-hop propagation (forged messages are rejected by non-cached nodes after first hop), causing resource waste rather than consensus breaks. Single-client bug affecting only Lodestar nodes. Does not meet BUG_BOUNTY_SCOPE High threshold (>33% network impact), but does meet Medium threshold as a protocol deviation exploitable via single P2P packet affecting >5% if Lodestar share exceeds that threshold.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: Dedup cache hit must not bypass signature verification on resubmitted items with different proofs. (forall item, seen_set: item.id in seen_set => verify_signature(item.sig) must still execute on new instance)
